conjecture_title: Conjecture: every forest of lanterns is pervasive
paper_title: Induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number. V. Chandeliers and strings
summary: The conjecture that every forest of lanterns is pervasive in the class of all graphs remains open. The source paper itself establishes the result for all ρ-controlled ideals (ρ ≥ 2) and for the class of string graphs (which is 2-controlled), but the question for the ideal of all graphs is explicitly noted as open in the abstract. A wide web search through 2026 found no subsequent paper resolving the full conjecture.

internal_refs_verified.note: Paper is about near-polynomial χ-boundedness for P_5-free graphs (Erdős–Hajnal direction); no mention of forest of lanterns or pervasiveness. False positive from corpus matching.
Paper is about Erdős–Hajnal loglog improvement; no connection to forest-of-lanterns conjecture. False positive from corpus matching.
WebFetch confirmed: paper addresses Gyárfás–Sumner conjecture (stable sets in H-free graphs), does not mention forest of lanterns or the pervasiveness conjecture from 1609.00314.
Paper concerns subdivisions and near-linear stable sets in the Gyárfás–Sumner direction; no connection to forest-of-lanterns pervasiveness conjecture. False positive from corpus matching.

WebFetch confirmed: paper disproves a conjecture about triangle-free induced subgraphs (attributed to Galvin–Rödl), does not mention forest of lanterns or arXiv:1609.00314. False positive from corpus matching.

notes: All five supplied internal references are false positives — none address the forest-of-lanterns pervasiveness conjecture. The conjecture is explicitly open in the source paper for the class of all graphs; the paper's own positive results (ρ-controlled ideals, string graphs) are the deepest known partial results. No follow-up resolving the full conjecture was found in the indexed literature through May 2026.
